cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out why the visitor came to John's house. The question is asking for the purpose of the visit. So, the story says that John is a writer who couldn't finish his story. He went to the cinema, and when he came back, someone had broken in. The visitor had a drink, smoked his cigarettes, read his story, and left a note. The note says the visitor didn't steal anything that night but mentioned being a thief and possibly returning if John becomes successful. The options are: steal something (A), read the story (B), have a drink (C), or visit the writer (D). Hmm. The visitor did have a drink and smoked, but those seem like incidental actions. The main thing is that the visitor read the story and gave suggestions. The note specifically mentions reading the story and providing feedback. The visitor's main purpose was to read the story and give input. The other actions (drinking, smoking) were probably just while they were there.
